free_tier: Hobby: unlimited static deploys; 100GB bandwidth/month; Serverless Functions 100GB-hrs
student_education_benefit: GitHub Student Pack: Pro plan free while student
benefits: Edge network; zero-config Vite deploy; instant preview URLs; SPA rewrites via vercel.json; Analytics built-in
drawbacks: Serverless function cold starts; vendor lock-in on edge features; hobby plan has 100GB bandwidth cap
recommendation_reason: Already configured (vercel.json + SPA rewrite exists); GitHub Student Pack Pro is free; zero migration cost; best DX for Vite+React

free_tier: Free: 100GB bandwidth/month; 300 build min/month; 1 concurrent build
benefits: Forms; split testing; identity; edge functions; generous free tier
drawbacks: Slower build times than Vercel for Vite; $10 student credit burns fast; no vercel.json equivalent so migration needed
recommendation_reason: Viable alternative if Vercel Student Pack expires; comparable free tier but requires removing vercel.json config

free_tier: Free: unlimited requests; unlimited bandwidth; 500 builds/month; Workers included
startup_credit: N/A — Workers free tier is generous
benefits: Unlimited bandwidth and requests; no egress fees; global CDN at 300+ PoPs; DDoS protection free; Workers for SSR
drawbacks: No vercel.json support (migration needed); Workers API differs from Vercel serverless; smaller ecosystem than Vercel
recommendation_reason: Best for zero-bandwidth-cost hosting at scale; no egress fees matter for India; good upgrade path if Vercel costs escalate

free_tier: Free: unlimited static sites; automatic TLS; global CDN
benefits: Simple deploy from Git; HTTPS automatic; works with Vite build
drawbacks: Less mature CDN than Vercel/Cloudflare; no edge functions; fewer integrations; smaller community
recommendation_reason: Static hosting is free but CDN performance in India is weaker than Vercel/Cloudflare; best as fallback only

free_tier: Free: 512MB RAM; spins down after 15min inactivity; 750 hrs/month
benefits: Simple Git-based deploy; PostgreSQL add-on; Redis add-on; zero DevOps; auto-HTTPS; preview environments
drawbacks: Free tier spins down (bad for APIs); $10 student credit runs out fast; no persistent disk on free
recommendation_reason: Easiest production path; GitHub Student Pack credit offsets first month; zero DevOps burden suits student team

free_tier: Free: $5/month credits included in Hobby plan; usage-based beyond that
benefits: Excellent DX; built-in PostgreSQL+Redis; usage-based pricing is fair; no cold starts; CLI is superb; monorepo support
drawbacks: Usage billing surprises; $5/month credit doesn't go far at scale; no Student Pack benefit
recommendation_reason: Best developer experience; built-in DB+cache avoids separate services; usage-based pricing cheapest for beta; most beginner-friendly

free_tier: Free: 3 shared-CPU-1x VMs; 3GB persistent volumes; 160GB outbound/month
benefits: True containerized deployment; persistent volumes; global regions including Singapore (nearest to India); no cold starts
drawbacks: Steeper learning curve (flyctl); requires Docker knowledge; Wireguard VPN for private networking; not beginner-friendly
recommendation_reason: Good for production scale; Singapore region reduces latency for India; requires more DevOps than Railway/Render

free_tier: Free: static sites only; Basic $5/month for apps
student_education_benefit: GitHub Student Pack: $200 credit for 1 year
benefits: Managed Kubernetes optional; $200 student credit covers ~3 months; good documentation; Bangalore region available
drawbacks: App Platform is basic compared to Railway; $5 Basic is limited (512MB RAM); needs upgrade for Node.js prod
recommendation_reason: $200 Student Pack credit is valuable for initial months; Bangalore data center gives low latency for India users

free_tier: Free: 2M requests/month; 360,000 vCPU-seconds; 180,000 GiB-seconds; 1GB egress/month
benefits: Scale to zero; pay per request; Mumbai region; integrates with Firebase/BigQuery; no idle cost
drawbacks: Cold starts on scale-to-zero; Docker required; GCP billing complexity; IAM setup overhead
recommendation_reason: $300 free credit covers 3+ months of beta; Mumbai region is best latency for India; pay-per-request model ideal for variable traffic

free_tier: Free: 0.5GB storage; 1 project; 10 branches; compute auto-suspends
student_education_benefit: GitHub Student Pack: Pro plan free for 1 year ($228 value)
benefits: Database branching (create DB clone per PR); serverless autoscaling; Postgres 16; Prisma-native integration; point-in-time restore
drawbacks: 0.5GB free tier storage is tight; auto-suspend has cold starts; relatively new service
recommendation_reason: GitHub Student Pack Pro (1 year) is outstanding value; branching feature perfect for team dev with shared PRs; Prisma + Neon is well-documented

free_tier: Free: 500MB DB; 1GB storage; 2 projects; 50MB file uploads
student_education_benefit: GitHub Student Pack: 1 year Pro free
benefits: Postgres + Auth + Storage + Edge Functions in one; Row Level Security; real-time subscriptions; dashboard is excellent
drawbacks: Prisma + Supabase requires connection pooling (PgBouncer); free tier pauses after 1 week inactivity; opinionated
recommendation_reason: Student Pack Pro covers entire beta; if team wants to replace custom auth + S3 storage later, Supabase is a single-platform path

free_tier: Included in Railway Hobby plan $5/month credit
benefits: Zero-config when using Railway for backend; same billing; no separate service; 1-click provision
drawbacks: Usage-based billing can surprise; no branching; less DX than Neon for DB-only usage
recommendation_reason: Best choice if using Railway for backend — single platform, single bill, zero extra config. Prisma works natively.

free_tier: Free: 90-day trial then expires (NOT permanently free)
benefits: Managed; automated backups on paid tier; works with Render backend
drawbacks: Free tier expires after 90 days (no permanent free); data deleted on expiry; limited to 1GB on Starter; read replicas are expensive
recommendation_reason: Only consider if hosting backend on Render; free tier expiry is a student trap — always set a reminder; Neon/Supabase are better DB choices

free_tier: Free: 5GB storage; 1 billion row reads/month
benefits: Branching like Git; great DX; free tier generous
drawbacks: MySQL dialect — incompatible with existing Prisma Postgres schema; would require rewriting all migrations
recommendation_reason: Schema is PostgreSQL-specific (Prisma). Migrating to MySQL requires rewriting all migrations and potentially query logic. Not viable.

free_tier: Free: 5GB storage; 20K GET; 2K PUT requests for 12 months
student_education_benefit: GitHub Student Pack: no direct S3 credit
benefits: Industry standard; multer-s3 integration ready; lifecycle policies; versioning; 11-nines durability; Mumbai (ap-south-1) region
drawbacks: Egress fees from India are expensive; complex IAM; 12-month free tier expires; no free permanent tier; costly at scale
recommendation_reason: multer-s3 is the de facto Node.js upload library; Mumbai region (ap-south-1) provides low latency for Indian users; start with free tier

free_tier: Free: 10GB storage/month; 1M Class A ops (PUT/POST); 10M Class B ops (GET); no egress fees
benefits: Zero egress fees (massive saving for India); S3-compatible API; multer-s3 works; no cold starts; workers integration
drawbacks: No CDN URL by default (need R2 public bucket or custom domain); slightly less ecosystem tooling than S3
recommendation_reason: Zero egress fees are best for India where data transfer costs are high; S3-compatible API means near-zero code change from S3; strongly recommended over S3 for cost

free_tier: Free: 1GB storage; 50MB max file size; included in Supabase free tier
student_education_benefit: GitHub Student Pack: 1 year Pro free
benefits: Integrated with Supabase Auth (RLS on storage); CDN included; image transformations
drawbacks: 1GB free is tight; max 50MB file on free; tightly coupled to Supabase ecosystem
recommendation_reason: Only compelling if already using Supabase for DB+Auth. Otherwise R2 or S3 are better standalone choices.

free_tier: Free: 10GB storage forever; 1GB download/day free
benefits: $0.006/GB storage (75% cheaper than S3); S3-compatible API; Cloudflare partnership eliminates egress when used with Cloudflare CDN
drawbacks: No Indian region (nearest is US/EU); S3-compatible but some edge cases; smaller ecosystem
recommendation_reason: 10GB free forever + Cloudflare CDN partnership (zero egress with Cloudflare) = best budget storage. Works with multer-s3.

free_tier: Free: 3K emails/month; 1 domain
benefits: Modern API; React Email templates; best DX of any email service; already referenced in code TODOs; TypeScript SDK
drawbacks: Not in GitHub Student Pack; 3K/month free is limited for scale; newer service with less track record than SendGrid
recommendation_reason: Already referenced in codebase TODOs (grep confirms); React Email + Resend is the modern Node.js email stack; 3K/month free covers all beta sends

free_tier: Free: 100 emails/month (test only)
benefits: Best deliverability rates in industry; transactional focus; detailed bounce analytics; message streams
drawbacks: 100 emails/month free is minimal; no Student Pack; more expensive than SendGrid/Resend at volume
recommendation_reason: Best deliverability for OTP-style transactional emails (therapy booking confirmations, password resets). Worth the cost for critical emails.

free_tier: Free: 100 emails/day forever (3K/month)
student_education_benefit: GitHub Student Pack: 1 year free Essentials (15K/month)
benefits: Generous free tier; Student Pack Essentials covers 15K/month; battle-tested; detailed analytics; Node.js SDK
drawbacks: Twilio acquisition caused product neglect; UI is dated; abuse (email spoofing) means deliverability can vary
recommendation_reason: GitHub Student Pack free Essentials (15K emails/month) covers entire beta with no cost. Fall back to Resend for DX.

free_tier: Free: 62K emails/month if sent from EC2/Lambda; otherwise $0.10/1K
benefits: Cheapest at scale ($0.10/1K); battle-tested; DKIM/SPF built-in
drawbacks: Complex sandbox setup requires domain verification + production access request; no good UI; need to implement bounce/complaint handling manually
recommendation_reason: Cheapest option at production scale but significant setup overhead. Not recommended for students; use Resend/SendGrid first and migrate to SES when cost matters.

free_tier: Free: 300 emails/day (9K/month); unlimited contacts
benefits: Marketing + transactional in one; SMS also available; good free tier; GDPR compliant (EU-based)
drawbacks: EU-centric (data stored in EU; latency concerns); no GitHub Student Pack; less DX focus than Resend
recommendation_reason: 9K emails/month free is generous for beta. Good if you also need SMS marketing. Otherwise Resend/SendGrid are better developer choices.

free_tier: Free: test dashboard; no monthly fee; pay per transaction only
benefits: Best India UPI/cards/netbanking support; instant settlements; already wired in codebase; INR-native; Razorpay Route for therapist payouts; dashboard excellent
drawbacks: 2% transaction fee; international cards 3%; need GST invoice
recommendation_reason: Already in codebase; best India payment coverage (UPI/PhonePe/GPay/cards/netbanking); Razorpay Route enables therapist commission splits. No alternative needed.

free_tier: Free: test mode; no monthly fee
benefits: International payments; excellent DX; Stripe Connect for marketplace; TypeScript types already in codebase
drawbacks: No UPI support; 2.9%+$0.30 per transaction is high in India; requires USD settlement for India pricing
recommendation_reason: Use for international clients (NRIs, foreign therapists); already has TypeScript types in codebase. Run Stripe + Razorpay in parallel: Razorpay for India, Stripe for international.

free_tier: Free: test sandbox; no monthly fee
benefits: Lower fees than Razorpay (1.75% vs 2%); UPI/cards/netbanking; good payouts API; Indian company; NBFC licensed
drawbacks: Less developer mindshare than Razorpay; smaller SDK ecosystem; fewer integrations
recommendation_reason: 0.25% lower fee than Razorpay becomes significant at scale (saves ₹2,500 on every ₹10L of GMV). Consider as Razorpay replacement at growth stage.

free_tier: Free: test mode; no monthly fee
benefits: Popular in India; LazyPay BNPL integration; EMI options; good for enterprise
drawbacks: Older API design; less modern DX than Razorpay; onboarding takes longer
recommendation_reason: More complex onboarding and older API makes it less suitable for student dev team. Use Razorpay first; consider PayU only if specific BNPL/EMI features are needed.

free_tier: Free: 10K participant-minutes/month; HD video; screen share; recording API
benefits: Already in codebase (API key commented); React hooks (@daily-co/daily-react); excellent REST API; Mumbai TURN servers; HIPAA-compliant plan available
drawbacks: 10K free minutes consumed by ~83 sessions/month; $0.00099/min after that; HIPAA plan costs extra
recommendation_reason: Already in codebase; HIPAA-ready tier matters for therapy; React SDK is well-maintained. Activate existing API key to go live.

free_tier: Free: 10K minutes/month; recording; chat; screen share
benefits: Better React SDK than Daily.co; lower latency India PoPs; live streaming support; whiteboard built-in
drawbacks: 10K min/month free is same as Daily.co; slightly more complex setup than Daily.co; newer company
recommendation_reason: Better React SDK DX and lower latency from India PoPs (Mumbai/Chennai). Viable migration from Daily.co with minimal code change. Consider as primary for new projects.

free_tier: Free: 10K minutes/month; video + audio
benefits: Largest WebRTC provider; extremely low latency; India PoPs; ML-based noise cancellation
drawbacks: Complex pricing matrix (video/audio/recording separate); SDK is C++ wrapper (less idiomatic); documentation harder to navigate; compliance docs complex
recommendation_reason: Industry leader for latency but complexity overhead not worth it for student team. Use Daily.co or 100ms which are purpose-built for therapy/education use cases.

free_tier: No meaningful free tier (extremely limited test room)
benefits: Twilio brand trust; HIPAA BAA available; Twilio ecosystem (SMS/email combined)
drawbacks: Most expensive option ($0.004/participant-minute vs $0.001 for competitors); no free tier; being deprecated in favor of Twilio Live
recommendation_reason: 4× more expensive than Daily.co/100ms with no compensating advantages. Being deprecated. Do not use.

free_tier: Self-host: FREE (open source); Cloud: 50K minutes/month free
benefits: Open source and self-hostable; cloud option; WebRTC + SFU; React SDK; growing ecosystem
drawbacks: Self-hosting requires managing SFU server; cloud is newer with less track record; HIPAA compliance unclear
recommendation_reason: Best option if the team wants no vendor lock-in. Self-host on Hetzner (₹1000/month) for full control. Good migration path from Daily.co.

free_tier: Free: $5 API credit for new accounts
benefits: Best quality reasoning; already in codebase; largest ecosystem; function calling; vision; DALL-E 3 access; fine-tuning
drawbacks: Most expensive frontier model; $2.50/1M input tokens; privacy concerns for sensitive therapy data; requires HIPAA BAA (Enterprise tier)
recommendation_reason: Already referenced in codebase for AI journaling/therapy; use GPT-4o for complex therapy support tasks only. Pair with GPT-4o-mini for moderation/classification.

free_tier: Free: $5 API credit for new accounts (shared with GPT-4o)
benefits: 93% cheaper than GPT-4o; good enough for moderation/classification/summaries; fast; already in codebase
drawbacks: Less capable for complex reasoning; not suitable for primary therapy AI
recommendation_reason: Use GPT-4o-mini for: content moderation, mood classification, journal summaries, slot suggestions. Reserve GPT-4o for therapy session AI. Massive cost saving.

free_tier: No free tier (API credits only on first signup)
benefits: Excellent reasoning; less likely to produce harmful content (better safety training); Constitutional AI; good for therapy context
drawbacks: No free tier; $0.80/1M input tokens; no Indian data residency; privacy compliance unclear for Indian healthcare
recommendation_reason: Better safety guardrails make Claude suitable for therapy AI where harmful outputs must be avoided. Use as fallback model or for sensitive therapy conversations.

free_tier: Free: 15 requests/min; 1M tokens/min; 1500 requests/day
startup_credit: $300 Google Cloud credit for new accounts
benefits: Extremely cheap ($0.075/1M input tokens); generous free tier; multimodal (text+image+audio); Google Cloud integration
drawbacks: Less established for conversational AI; model behavior less predictable than OpenAI/Claude; Google privacy terms for Indian data
recommendation_reason: Best cost-per-token if quality is acceptable. Use for non-critical features (horoscope generation, mood trend summaries) where premium model quality isn't required.

free_tier: Free: 14.4K tokens/min on Llama 3.1 70B; 30 req/min
benefits: Extremely fast inference (10× faster than OpenAI); Llama 3.1 70B open weights; very cheap; low latency
drawbacks: Rate limits on free tier; Llama models slightly lower quality than GPT-4o for complex reasoning; no vision model; dependent on Groq hardware availability
recommendation_reason: Best for real-time chat features where latency matters (astrology chat, live journaling suggestions). Groq + Llama 3.1 70B free tier can handle development entirely.

free_tier: Free: 1M events/month; 5K session recordings; feature flags; 1 year data retention
benefits: Open source (self-hostable); product analytics + session recording + feature flags + A/B testing + heatmaps in one; GDPR-friendly (self-host); React SDK
drawbacks: Self-hosting requires DevOps; 1M free events sounds like a lot but can go fast with tracking everything; EU data residency default
recommendation_reason: Best analytics for startups: open source + generous free tier + GitHub Student Pack credit. Session recording helps identify UX issues in therapy booking funnel. Privacy-sensitive (HIPAA): self-host or use EU cloud.

free_tier: No free tier (14-day trial); $9/month for up to 10K monthly pageviews
benefits: GDPR-compliant by design; no cookies; lightweight (< 1KB script vs GA4's 45KB); perfect for privacy-sensitive platforms; EU-hosted
drawbacks: No free tier; limited funnel/cohort analysis vs PostHog; pageview-based pricing gets expensive at scale
recommendation_reason: Ideal for Soul Yatri's privacy-sensitive wellness data. GDPR/DPDPA compliance built-in. No cookie banner needed. Low script weight improves page speed scores.

free_tier: Free: 20M events/month; unlimited data history; cohort analysis
benefits: Powerful funnel analysis; cohort retention; A/B experiments; 20M free events is huge
drawbacks: Complex to set up properly; data governance for DPDPA compliance unclear; can send data to US servers by default
recommendation_reason: 20M events/month free is the most generous free tier. Good for deep funnel analysis (therapy booking, subscription conversion). But setup complexity is higher than PostHog.

free_tier: Free forever; unlimited events
benefits: Free; most widely used; integration with Google Ads/Search Console; BigQuery export free
drawbacks: Sends data to Google (GDPR/DPDPA concern for mental health platform); complex event model; data sampling at scale; India data may be stored in US
recommendation_reason: Mental health/therapy data must not be shared with Google. DPDPA 2023 (India's privacy law) and PDPB considerations make GA4 a compliance risk for Soul Yatri.

free_tier: Free: 10M events/month; unlimited sources; 1 year retention
benefits: Best-in-class behavioral cohort analysis; Amplitude Charts are excellent; strong product analytics
drawbacks: 10M events/month free is generous; but Enterprise tier pricing is opaque; GDPR compliance needs configuration
recommendation_reason: 10M free events covers initial production. Better behavioral analytics than PostHog for understanding therapy booking funnels. Consider if PostHog free tier is exceeded.

free_tier: Free: 5K errors/month; 10K performance units; 1 user
student_education_benefit: GitHub Student Pack: 500K Sentry errors ($26 value)
benefits: Industry standard for error monitoring; source maps; performance tracing; session replay; React + Node.js SDKs; already the de facto standard
drawbacks: 500K errors/month in Student Pack burns fast if misconfigured; can be noisy; source map upload requires CI setup
recommendation_reason: Industry standard; GitHub Student Pack 500K errors covers entire beta and early production. React + Express SDKs require minimal setup. Must-have for any production app.

free_tier: Free: 1GB logs/month; 3-day log retention; 5 monitors; 10 alerts
benefits: Logs + uptime monitoring + incident management in one; beautiful UI; Pino integration (if using Pino logger)
drawbacks: Less powerful error tracking than Sentry; more of a log aggregation + uptime tool
recommendation_reason: Best complement to Sentry: BetterStack for uptime monitoring (free tier) + Sentry for error tracking. Use BetterStack's free uptime monitoring from day one.

free_tier: Free: 1K sessions/month; 1 month data retention
benefits: Session replay + error tracking + network request logging; see exactly what user did before an error; React DevTools integration
drawbacks: Very expensive Pro tier ($99/month); 1K sessions/month free is limited; privacy concern (records user sessions — sensitive for therapy platform)
recommendation_reason: Session replay is powerful for debugging UX issues in therapy booking flow. BUT recording sessions on a mental health platform raises serious privacy concerns. Proceed with caution and explicit user consent.

free_tier: Free: 5K events/month; 30-day retention; 1 user
benefits: Real-time error alerting; deploy tracking; fingerprinting; good Node.js SDK
drawbacks: Less powerful than Sentry; smaller community; UI is dated
recommendation_reason: Viable Sentry alternative at lower cost. 5K/month free is same as Sentry but without Student Pack benefit. Choose Sentry over Rollbar for Student Pack credit.

free_tier: Free: 0.5TB ingest/month; 30-day retention
benefits: Next-gen log analytics; APL query language; very fast; Vercel integration native
drawbacks: More of a log analytics tool than error monitoring; requires custom instrumentation; not a drop-in Sentry replacement
recommendation_reason: Excellent complement to Sentry on Vercel deployments — Axiom has native Vercel log drain integration. Use Axiom for structured logging + Sentry for error tracking.

free_tier: Free: fully implemented
benefits: HS256 access (15min) + refresh (7d httpOnly cookie); custom role support; full control; no vendor dependency; already live and tested
drawbacks: Maintenance burden; no social login (Google/Apple) out of box; MFA not implemented; security audit needed
recommendation_reason: Well-implemented custom JWT is a strength, not a weakness. Access token in-memory + httpOnly refresh cookie follows security best practices. Keep custom auth; add social login via Passport.js if needed.

free_tier: Free: 10K MAU; unlimited orgs; MFA; social logins
benefits: Prebuilt UI components; social login (Google/Apple); MFA; user management dashboard; React hooks
drawbacks: Would require major refactor of all auth routes; replaces existing JWT system; less control; MAU-based pricing expensive at scale
recommendation_reason: Only consider Clerk if the team wants to completely remove auth boilerplate. Given custom JWT is well-implemented, migration cost exceeds benefit for current stage.

free_tier: Free: unlimited MAU; social providers; email OTP
student_education_benefit: GitHub Student Pack: 1 year Pro free
benefits: Social login; Row Level Security integration; magic links; phone OTP; free with Supabase DB
drawbacks: Tightly coupled to Supabase ecosystem; migrating away from Supabase Auth is painful; less control than custom JWT
recommendation_reason: Only adopt if moving entire DB to Supabase. Don't adopt Supabase Auth alone — too much coupling. Custom JWT + Supabase DB is a valid hybrid.

free_tier: Free: 7500 MAU; 2 social connections
benefits: Industry standard; enterprise SSO; HIPAA BAA available; MFA; anomaly detection
drawbacks: 7500 MAU free then expensive ($23+); overkill for current stage; heavy SDK; complex setup; vendor lock-in
recommendation_reason: 7500 MAU cap is hit quickly; $23+/month for a student project replaces a working auth system. Custom JWT is better for this stage. Auth0 makes sense at enterprise scale.

free_tier: Free: open source; self-hosted
benefits: TypeScript-native; Prisma adapter exists; social login plugins; MFA plugin; session management; growing ecosystem; no vendor lock-in
drawbacks: Relatively new (2024); smaller community than Auth0/Clerk; fewer tutorials; API may change
recommendation_reason: Worth watching as a future migration path from custom JWT. Prisma adapter means near-zero schema change. If custom JWT becomes a maintenance burden, Better Auth is the modern open-source replacement.

free_tier: Upstash Redis free: 10K commands/day; 256MB; 1 database
benefits: Industry standard; already referenced in code TODOs; retry logic; delayed jobs; priority queues; cron jobs; BullMQ board for monitoring
drawbacks: Requires Redis (Upstash or self-hosted); BullMQ adds complexity vs in-memory; 10K commands/day free is limited
recommendation_reason: Already referenced in codebase TODOs. Upstash serverless Redis free tier covers dev; pay-as-you-go at beta. BullMQ is the standard for Node.js background jobs (email sends, video session cleanup, AI batch processing).

free_tier: Free: 50K job runs/month; 10 concurrent; 10 event sources
benefits: Excellent DX; TypeScript-native; no Redis required; retry UI; real-time logs; scheduled tasks; GitHub Student Pack access
drawbacks: Newer platform (2023); vendor dependency; less ecosystem than BullMQ; cloud-only (no self-host free)
recommendation_reason: GitHub Student Pack access + no Redis requirement makes this the easiest background job solution. Perfect for therapy booking confirmations, scheduled reminders, AI batch processing.

free_tier: Free: 50K function runs/month; 3-day event log retention
benefits: TypeScript-first; event-driven; durable functions; step functions for complex workflows; great for multi-step therapy booking flows
drawbacks: Vendor dependency; event-driven model is a paradigm shift from BullMQ
recommendation_reason: Best for complex multi-step workflows (book therapy → send confirmation → create video room → notify therapist). Durable functions handle partial failures elegantly. 50K/month free covers beta.

free_tier: Free: 500 requests/day; 5 schedules; 10K messages/month
benefits: HTTP-based message queue; no server to manage; works with serverless; same Upstash account as Redis
drawbacks: 500 requests/day free is very limited; HTTP-based means latency; not suitable for high-frequency jobs
recommendation_reason: Use QStash for simple webhook delivery and scheduled tasks (daily horoscope push, weekly report emails). Not suitable for high-frequency video session management jobs.

free_tier: Deprecated — no longer maintained
benefits: Was a simple job queue for Next.js/Vercel
drawbacks: DEPRECATED — project archived; no maintenance; security vulnerabilities unpatched
recommendation_reason: Project is archived and deprecated. The maintainer recommends Inngest as replacement. Do not use.

free_tier: Free: open source; self-hosted
benefits: Lightweight; already implemented server-side; works on any Node.js host; no vendor dependency; full control
drawbacks: Manual reconnection logic needed client-side; no built-in rooms/namespaces; no presence; not wired to UI yet
recommendation_reason: ws is already implemented and works well for current needs (real-time therapy session events). Connect it to React UI before considering upgrades. Add reconnection logic with ws heartbeat pattern.

free_tier: Free: open source; self-hosted
benefits: Rooms; namespaces; automatic reconnection; fallback to long-polling; larger ecosystem than ws; client library handles edge cases
drawbacks: Larger bundle size; abstraction over ws; requires Socket.io-compatible client (not plain ws)
recommendation_reason: Natural upgrade from ws if reconnection/rooms become painful to manage manually. Minimal migration effort — swap ws server for Socket.io server. Recommended for video therapy session signaling.

free_tier: Free: 6M messages/month; 200 concurrent connections
benefits: Managed WebSocket infrastructure; 99.999% SLA; presence channels; history; pub/sub; push notifications; GitHub Student Pack 1 year free
drawbacks: Vendor dependency; ₹29/month after free tier; for most use cases ws/Socket.io is sufficient
recommendation_reason: GitHub Student Pack 1 year free is compelling. Use Ably if the team wants managed WebSocket without DevOps burden. Real-time presence ('therapist is typing') features work out of the box.

free_tier: Free: 200K messages/day; 100 concurrent connections
benefits: Channels + Beams (push notifications); battle-tested; GitHub Student Pack access; easy to integrate
drawbacks: Expensive Pro tier; 200K messages/day sounds like a lot but isn't at scale; vendor lock-in
recommendation_reason: GitHub Student Pack + 200K messages/day free is generous for beta. Good if you also need mobile push notifications (Beams) in the same SDK.

free_tier: Free: with Supabase free tier; 200 concurrent connections
student_education_benefit: GitHub Student Pack: 1 year Pro free
benefits: Postgres Change Data Capture → real-time subscriptions; presence; broadcast; integrated with Supabase Auth RLS
drawbacks: Tightly coupled to Supabase ecosystem; not useful without Supabase DB
recommendation_reason: Only relevant if migrating DB to Supabase. Supabase Realtime replaces ws for DB change notifications. Powerful for real-time therapy notes sync.

free_tier: Free: unlimited bandwidth; global CDN; DDoS protection; SSL; DNS; WAF (limited)
benefits: Global CDN with India PoPs (Mumbai/Delhi/Chennai); DDoS mitigation; free SSL; DNS with sub-ms resolution; R2 integration; Workers; DNSSEC
drawbacks: Some advanced security features (bot management) require Pro/Business; Workers are separate pricing
recommendation_reason: No-brainer: free CDN + DDoS protection + SSL + DNS from day one. Every Soul Yatri deployment should be behind Cloudflare regardless of other choices. Mumbai PoP ensures <50ms latency for Indian users.

free_tier: Free: 1TB data out/month; 10M HTTP(S) requests for 12 months (first year only)
benefits: Tightly integrated with S3/EC2/Route53; Lambda@Edge; Origin Shield; custom SSL certs via ACM
drawbacks: 12-month free tier expires; India egress is expensive ($0.170/GB from Mumbai); complex pricing; overkill if not on AWS stack
recommendation_reason: Only choose CloudFront if hosting on AWS (S3+EC2). For India-specific deployments, Cloudflare's Mumbai PoP offers better latency and zero cost.

free_tier: Free: 14-day trial; 1TB free with GitHub Student Pack
benefits: Cheapest CDN ($0.005/GB); India PoPs; Storage zones; image optimizer; stream (video); very fast
drawbacks: Less feature-rich than Cloudflare; no WAF; no DDoS protection at free tier level; less ecosystem
recommendation_reason: GitHub Student Pack 1TB free is useful. Best budget CDN for media assets (audio/video recordings). Use Cloudflare for DNS/security + Bunny.net for asset delivery to save on bandwidth costs.

free_tier: No meaningful free tier (enterprise-focused)
benefits: Instant purge; edge computing; real-time log streaming; excellent SLA
drawbacks: Enterprise pricing only; no free tier; overkill for student project; minimum contract requirements
recommendation_reason: Enterprise CDN with enterprise pricing. No free tier. Cloudflare provides equivalent (or better) features at zero cost for this scale.

free_tier: Free: $15 trial credit (test only; cannot send to unverified numbers)
benefits: International standard; reliable India delivery; GitHub Student Pack $50 credit; MMS; WhatsApp API
drawbacks: ₹0.664/SMS to India is expensive vs Indian providers; DLT registration still required; trial restricts to verified numbers
recommendation_reason: GitHub Student Pack $50 credit covers ~7500 OTPs for testing. Good for dev/testing but switch to MSG91 or Fast2SMS for production to save 90%+ on SMS costs.

free_tier: No free tier; DLT registration required before any sends
benefits: Best India SMS provider; OTP-specific API; flow builder; DLT-compliant out of box; WhatsApp API; ₹0.20/OTP
drawbacks: DLT registration is mandatory (TRAI) and takes 1-3 weeks; minimum recharge required; no free tier
recommendation_reason: Best production SMS provider for India. DLT-compliant, reliable, cheap (₹0.20/OTP). Start DLT registration early (3+ weeks lead time). Use Twilio Student Pack credit for dev testing while waiting for DLT approval.

free_tier: Free: ₹50 signup credit (~200 SMS)
benefits: Cheapest India SMS (₹0.03-0.25/SMS depending on route); quick onboarding; DLT route available; promotional and transactional SMS
drawbacks: DLT route required for transactional OTP; non-DLT route limited; less reliable than MSG91 for OTP delivery; support can be slow
recommendation_reason: ₹50 free signup credit gets you started immediately. ₹0.03/SMS promotional route for testing; ₹0.25/SMS DLT transactional for production OTPs. Best budget choice for student team.

free_tier: Free: limited trial on signup
benefits: OTP-only focus; voice OTP fallback; DLT compliant; very reliable delivery for India OTP; simple REST API
drawbacks: OTP-focused only (no marketing SMS); pricing similar to MSG91; smaller ecosystem
recommendation_reason: Best-in-class OTP delivery for India. Voice OTP fallback (for users who can't receive SMS) is unique differentiator — critical for elderly therapy clients who may prefer voice confirmation.

free_tier: Free: 50GB metrics/month; 50GB logs/month; 10K traces/month; 50GB Loki logs
benefits: Full observability stack (Grafana + Prometheus + Loki + Tempo + k6); dashboards; alerting; Node.js metrics exporter easy
drawbacks: Setup requires metrics instrumentation; 50GB free sounds like a lot but compresses well; more DevOps than Sentry
recommendation_reason: Best choice for infrastructure monitoring at scale. Grafana + Prometheus Node.js exporter can track API latency, DB query times, WebSocket connections. Free tier is generous.

free_tier: Free: 100GB data ingest/month; 1 full platform user; unlimited basic users
benefits: APM + logs + infrastructure + errors + synthetics in one; 100GB/month free is very generous; Node.js APM agent; React Browser agent
drawbacks: 100GB free can be exceeded at scale; APM agent adds overhead; complex to configure properly; US data residency default
recommendation_reason: 100GB/month free covers most production workloads. Node.js APM agent auto-instruments Express.js routes — see exactly which API endpoints are slowest. Good Sentry complement.

free_tier: Free: open source (self-host); Cloud: 1M spans/month free
benefits: OpenTelemetry-native; distributed tracing; metrics; logs; open source self-hostable; excellent for microservices
drawbacks: Newer platform; smaller community; requires OpenTelemetry instrumentation; self-hosting adds overhead
recommendation_reason: OpenTelemetry standard means no vendor lock-in. If Soul Yatri adds microservices (auth service, video service, AI service), distributed tracing via Uptrace is valuable.

free_tier: Free: 14-day trial only; no permanent free tier
benefits: Industry-leading APM; DogStatsD; 450+ integrations; ML anomaly detection; compliance dashboards
drawbacks: No free tier after trial; $15/host/month minimum; at 3+ hosts = $45/month minimum; overkill for student project
recommendation_reason: No free tier after 14-day trial. Enterprise APM pricing ($15+/host/month) is unjustifiable for a student project. Use Sentry (error monitoring) + Grafana Cloud (metrics) for equivalent coverage at zero cost.
